dialogos.texto_en: I had so much fun snorkeling! The fish were so cute.
I didn't know the underwater world\ncould be so beautiful either.
What are you two talking about?
Oh, Fu-kun.
We're talking about\nhow much fun snorkeling was.
Oh yeah, I had a great time too.\nI got to see a lot of fish up close.
Yeah! It was amazing!\nIt felt like we were in a dream world!
I see! If Raiha was that happy,\nthen it was worth suggesting it!
You suggested it just for her, didn't you?
Ooh...
I overheard you talking to Itsuki.\nIt was for her, wasn't it?
Well, yeah.
Teehee!\nSorry, I was being mean.\nI didn't mean it that way.
Don't give me such a negative look.\nEveryone had a good time, including me.
I see. I'm glad, then.
Well, it's good that you're able to be\nso considerate like this now, big bro...
I can't believe how much you've grown\nfrom how you used to be. It's a relief!
Th-that's enough about that.\nSo, what were you guys doing?
We were checking out other activities we could do.
Like, there's banana boat rides and all sorts of things.
Wow, I see.\nYou could stay here all day and not get bored.
Snorkeling was so much fun,\nbut I'd like to try scuba diving next.
Scuba diving is more advanced,\nso it might not be possible this time.
But there are other interesting activities too.\nLike a tour to an uninhabited island by sea kayak.
Oh, really? They have something like that, huh.
Yeah, but I guess it's already fully booked.
What kind of activity is it?
Well, it seems like we can ride a sea kayak\nand go to a nearby island.
We can also go into the limestone caves\nalong the coast on the way.\nSeems like there's a lot to do.
Entering the limestone caves? Sounds like an adventure!
Yes, it sounds fun.\nI would have liked to try it\nif the reservations weren't already full.
That's a bit surprising.\nFor you to make that suggestion.
You don't get it, do you, Fu-kun.\nIt's this kind of place.\nYou have to enjoy it, or it's a waste.
I fully enjoy anything that seems fun.\nI never slack off in anything I do.
Ah! Look, big bro.\nThese kayaks are for two people.
And?
You like this kind of stuff too, right, Raiha?
Yeah, it sounds fun.\nIt's an adventure, just as you said, big bro.
Yotsuba also likes this kind of thing, doesn't she?
I know.\nHer kayak would probably move the fastest.
Besides, she seems like she could paddle alone\nand go faster.\nIt might be dangerous to take her with us.
Haha, in a way, it might have been a good thing\nthat the reservations were full.
Ah! Look, big bro.\nThese kayaks are for two people.
And?
This gets me so pumped!\nI'm going to see a sight I don't usually get to!
Fu-kun, you surprisingly like this kind of thing,\ndon't you? Even though you're an indoor person.
E-even indoor people yearn for romance,\nyou know. Is that so bad?
I didn't say it was bad.\nRomance is always the driving force that moves people.
...Yeah, you're right.
(When Nino says it, it sounds convincing...)
Ah! Look, big bro.\nThese kayaks are for two people.
And?
It's a chance for you to ride with Itsuki-san\nand show off your cool paddling skills.
Yeah, um... I wonder?
Not suitable for you, Fu-kun.\nThat won't make a good impression.
If you're unable to make it move forward\nafter paddling and paddling,\nyou won't be able to show your face in public.
Seriously...?\nIf I can't make the kayak move,\nthen she...
Hey, wait a minute?\nWhat did you just imagine right now?
Ah...\nWell...
Ugh, my LORD!\nYou really have no subtlety! You're the worst!
I-I'm so sorry.
Listen, there are things that you can and can't\nsay, even as a boyfriend. You'd best be careful, okay?
And before you talk about that to Itsuki,\nyou should take care\nof your own strength and physical stamina.
You can't even carry rice by yourself.\nHow are you going to live alone?
...I'll just have it delivered.
Fu-kun?
I-it's nothing!\nI'll think about my physical strength!
Be strong, big bro.
